question: why push away need to be straight and not to the outside?
content:
let’s explain the importance of the direction of the "pushaway" in bowling. the initial movement when you start your swing. 
if, instead of moving the ball straight forward toward your target, you push it slightly to the side of your body, several problems can occur:
the ball moves behind your body during the backswing.
your swing path becomes too round or circular.
this leads to poor aiming and excessive spin (over-spin) on the release.
this is considered the "wrong" way because it makes your shots less accurate and harder to control.
what’s the “right” way?
the pushaway should be aligned with your body and directed straight toward your target.
it can be sent slightly inside (inside your eyed target) to create a modern swing style, where the ball stays outside the body at the top of the backswing.
by not overusing your muscles, the swing will naturally reposition itself close to your ankle for a proper release, with your hand under the ball.
why does this matter?
alignment and direction: keeping the pushaway straight ensures your swing path is consistent and aimed at your target, improving accuracy.
swing shape: a straight pushaway leads to a more natural, repeatable swing, while a sideways pushaway causes a rounder, less controlled swing.
release quality: proper alignment helps you release the ball with your hand under it, which is ideal for control and power.
